Examples.Title: Chat API (v1)
Examples.Description:
Generates a text response to a user message.
To learn how to use the Chat API and RAG follow our [Text Generation guides](https://docs.cohere.com/docs/chat-api).
Examples.Code:
### Chat API (v1)
# @name chat
POST {{host}}/v1/chat
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
X-Client-Name: my-cool-project
Accepts: text/event-stream
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

{
  "message": "string",
  "model": "string",
  "stream": true,
  "preamble": "string",
  "chat_history": [
    {
      "role": "CHATBOT",
      "message": "string",
      "tool_calls": []
    }
  ],
  "conversation_id": "string",
  "prompt_truncation": "OFF",
  "connectors": [
    {
      "id": "string",
      "user_access_token": "string",
      "continue_on_failure": true,
      "options": {}
    }
  ],
  "search_queries_only": true,
  "documents": [
    {
      "id": "string"
    }
  ],
  "citation_quality": "ENABLED",
  "temperature": 0,
  "max_tokens": 0,
  "max_input_tokens": 0,
  "k": 0,
  "p": 0.75,
  "seed": 0,
  "stop_sequences": [
    "string"
  ],
  "frequency_penalty": 0,
  "presence_penalty": 0,
  "raw_prompting": true,
  "tools": [
    {
      "name": "string",
      "description": "string",
      "parameter_definitions": {}
    }
  ],
  "tool_results": [
    {
      "call": {},
      "outputs": []
    }
  ],
  "force_single_step": true,
  "response_format": {
    "type": "text"
  },
  "safety_mode": "CONTEXTUAL"
}

## Responses
# 200
# Description: OK
# Content-Type: application/json
# 400
# Description: This error is returned when the request is not well formed. This could be because:
  - JSON is invalid
  - The request is missing required fields
  - The request contains an invalid combination of fields

# Content-Type: application/json
# 401
# Description: This error indicates that the operation attempted to be performed is not allowed. This could be because:
  - The api token is invalid
  - The user does not have the necessary permissions

# Content-Type: application/json
# 403
# Description: This error indicates that the operation attempted to be performed is not allowed. This could be because:
  - The api token is invalid
  - The user does not have the necessary permissions

# Content-Type: application/json
# 404
# Description: This error is returned when a resource is not found. This could be because:
  - The endpoint does not exist
  - The resource does not exist eg model id, dataset id

# Content-Type: application/json
# 422
# Description: This error is returned when the request is not well formed. This could be because:
  - JSON is invalid
  - The request is missing required fields
  - The request contains an invalid combination of fields

# Content-Type: application/json
# 429
# Description: Too many requests
# Content-Type: application/json
# 498
# Description: This error is returned when a request or response contains a deny-listed token.

# Content-Type: application/json
# 499
# Description: This error is returned when a request is cancelled by the user.

# Content-Type: application/json
# 500
# Description: This error is returned when an uncategorised internal server error occurs.

# Content-Type: application/json
# 501
# Description: This error is returned when the requested feature is not implemented.

# Content-Type: application/json
# 503
# Description: This error is returned when the service is unavailable. This could be due to:
  - Too many users trying to access the service at the same time

# Content-Type: application/json
# 504
# Description: This error is returned when a request to the server times out. This could be due to:
  - An internal services taking too long to respond

# Content-Type: application/json

Examples.Title: Check API key
Examples.Description: Checks that the api key in the Authorization header is valid and active
Examples.Setup: This example assumes `using Cohere;` is in scope and `apiKey` contains the required credential.

Examples.Title: Detokenize
Examples.Description: This endpoint takes tokens using byte-pair encoding and returns their text representation. To learn more about tokenization and byte pair encoding, see the tokens page.
Examples.Code:
### Detokenize
# @name detokenize
POST {{host}}/v1/detokenize
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
X-Client-Name: my-cool-project
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

{
  "tokens": [
    0
  ],
  "model": "string"
}

Examples.Title: Embed API (v1)
Examples.Description:
This endpoint returns text and image embeddings. An embedding is a list of floating point numbers that captures semantic information about the content that it represents.

Embeddings can be used to create classifiers as well as empower semantic search. To learn more about embeddings, see the embedding page.

If you want to learn more how to use the embedding model, have a look at the [Semantic Search Guide](https://docs.cohere.com/docs/semantic-search).
Examples.Code:
### Embed API (v1)
# @name embed
POST {{host}}/v1/embed
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
X-Client-Name: my-cool-project
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

{
  "texts": [
    "string"
  ],
  "images": [
    "string"
  ],
  "model": "string",
  "input_type": "search_document",
  "embedding_types": [
    "float"
  ],
  "truncate": "END"
}

Examples.Title: Rerank API (v1)
Examples.Description: This endpoint takes in a query and a list of texts and produces an ordered array with each text assigned a relevance score.
Examples.Code:
### Rerank API (v1)
# @name rerank
POST {{host}}/v1/rerank
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
X-Client-Name: my-cool-project
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

{
  "model": "string",
  "query": "string",
  "documents": [
    "string"
  ],
  "top_n": 0,
  "rank_fields": [
    "string"
  ],
  "return_documents": false,
  "max_chunks_per_doc": 10
}

Examples.Title: Tokenize
Examples.Description: This endpoint splits input text into smaller units called tokens using byte-pair encoding (BPE). To learn more about tokenization and byte pair encoding, see the tokens page.
Examples.Setup: This example assumes `using Cohere;` is in scope and `apiKey` contains the required credential.

Examples.Title: Cancel a batch
Examples.Description: Cancels an in-progress batch
Examples.Code:
### Cancel a batch
# @name CancelBatch
POST {{host}}/v2/batches/{{id}}:cancel
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
X-Client-Name: my-cool-project
Accept: application/json

## Responses
# 200
# Description: A successful response.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 400
# Description: Bad Request
# Content-Type: application/json
# 401
# Description: Unauthorized
# Content-Type: application/json
# 403
# Description: Forbidden
# Content-Type: application/json
# 404
# Description: Not Found
# Content-Type: application/json
# 500
# Description: Internal Server Error
# Content-Type: application/json
# 503
# Description: Status Service Unavailable
# Content-Type: application/json

Examples.Title: Create a batch
Examples.Description: Creates and executes a batch from an uploaded dataset of requests
Examples.Code:
### Create a batch
# @name CreateBatch
POST {{host}}/v2/batches
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
X-Client-Name: my-cool-project
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

{
  "id": "string",
  "name": "string",
  "creator_id": "string",
  "org_id": "string",
  "status": "BATCH_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED",
  "created_at": "2025-01-01T00:00:00Z",
  "updated_at": "2025-01-01T00:00:00Z",
  "input_dataset_id": "string",
  "output_dataset_id": "string",
  "input_tokens": "string",
  "output_tokens": "string",
  "model": "string",
  "num_records": 0,
  "num_successful_records": 0,
  "num_failed_records": 0,
  "status_reason": "string"
}

## Responses
# 200
# Description: A successful response.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 400
# Description: Bad Request
# Content-Type: application/json
# 401
# Description: Unauthorized
# Content-Type: application/json
# 403
# Description: Forbidden
# Content-Type: application/json
# 404
# Description: Not Found
# Content-Type: application/json
# 500
# Description: Internal Server Error
# Content-Type: application/json
# 503
# Description: Status Service Unavailable
# Content-Type: application/json

Examples.Title: Create a Dataset
Examples.Description: Create a dataset by uploading a file. See ['Dataset Creation'](https://docs.cohere.com/docs/datasets#dataset-creation) for more information.
Examples.Code:
### Create a Dataset
# @name create-dataset
POST {{host}}/v1/datasets?name={{name}}&type=embed-input&keep_original_file={{keep_original_file}}&skip_malformed_input={{skip_malformed_input}}&keep_fields={{keep_fields}}&optional_fields={{optional_fields}}&text_separator={{text_separator}}&csv_delimiter={{csv_delimiter}}
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
X-Client-Name: my-cool-project
Content-Type: multipart/form-data
Accept: application/json

Examples.Title: Delete a Dataset
Examples.Description: Delete a dataset by ID. Datasets are automatically deleted after 30 days, but they can also be deleted manually.
Examples.Code:

Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
X-Client-Name: my-cool-project

Examples.Title: Get Dataset Usage
Examples.Description: View the dataset storage usage for your Organization. Each Organization can have up to 10GB of storage across all their users.
Examples.Setup: This example assumes `using Cohere;` is in scope and `apiKey` contains the required credential.

Examples.Title: List Datasets
Examples.Description: List datasets that have been created.
Examples.Setup: This example assumes `using Cohere;` is in scope and `apiKey` contains the required credential.

Examples.Title: Cancel an Embed Job
Examples.Description: This API allows users to cancel an active embed job. Once invoked, the embedding process will be terminated, and users will be charged for the embeddings processed up to the cancellation point. It's important to note that partial results will not be available to users after cancellation.
Examples.Code:
### Cancel an Embed Job
# @name cancel-embed-job
POST {{host}}/v1/embed-jobs/{{id}}/cancel
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
X-Client-Name: my-cool-project
Accept: application/json

## Responses
# 200
# Description: OK
# 400

  - The user does not have the necessary permissions

  - The resource does not exist eg model id, dataset id

Examples.Title: Create an Embed Job
Examples.Description: This API launches an async Embed job for a [Dataset](https://docs.cohere.com/docs/datasets) of type `embed-input`. The result of a completed embed job is new Dataset of type `embed-output`, which contains the original text entries and the corresponding embeddings.
Examples.Code:
### Create an Embed Job
# @name create-embed-job
POST {{host}}/v1/embed-jobs
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
X-Client-Name: my-cool-project
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

{
  "model": "string",
  "dataset_id": "string",
  "input_type": "search_document",
  "name": "string",
  "embedding_types": [
    "float"
  ],
  "truncate": "END"
}

## Responses
# 200
# Description: OK
# Content-Type: application/json
# 400
# Description: This error is returned when the request is not well formed. This could be because:
  - JSON is invalid
  - The request is missing required fields
  - The request contains an invalid combination of fields

# Content-Type: application/json
# 401
# Description: This error indicates that the operation attempted to be performed is not allowed. This could be because:
  - The api token is invalid
  - The user does not have the necessary permissions

# Content-Type: application/json
# 403

Examples.Title: List Embed Jobs
Examples.Description: The list embed job endpoint allows users to view all embed jobs history for that specific user.
Examples.Setup: This example assumes `using Cohere;` is in scope and `apiKey` contains the required credential.

Examples.Title: Chat API (v2)
Examples.Description:
Generates a text response to a user message and streams it down, token by token. To learn how to use the Chat API with streaming follow our [Text Generation guides](https://docs.cohere.com/v2/docs/chat-api).

Follow the [Migration Guide](https://docs.cohere.com/v2/docs/migrating-v1-to-v2) for instructions on moving from API v1 to API v2.
Examples.Code:
### Chat API (v2)
# @name chatv2
POST {{host}}/v2/chat
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
X-Client-Name: my-cool-project
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

{
  "stream": true,
  "model": "string",
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": "string"
    }
  ],
  "tools": [
    {
      "type": "function",
      "function": {}
    }
  ],
  "strict_tools": true,
  "documents": [
    "string"
  ],
  "citation_options": {
    "mode": "ENABLED"
  },
  "response_format": {
    "type": "text"
  },
  "safety_mode": "CONTEXTUAL",
  "max_tokens": 0,
  "stop_sequences": [
    "string"
  ],
  "temperature": 0,
  "seed": 0,
  "frequency_penalty": 0,
  "presence_penalty": 0,
  "k": 0,
  "p": 0.75,
  "logprobs": true,
  "tool_choice": "REQUIRED",
  "thinking": {
    "type": "enabled",
    "token_budget": 0
  },
  "priority": 0
}

## Responses
# 200
# Description: OK
# Content-Type: application/json
# 400
# Description: This error is returned when the request is not well formed. This could be because:
  - JSON is invalid
  - The request is missing required fields
  - The request contains an invalid combination of fields

# Content-Type: application/json
# 401
# Description: This error indicates that the operation attempted to be performed is not allowed. This could be because:
  - The api token is invalid
  - The user does not have the necessary permissions

# Content-Type: application/json
# 403
# Description: This error indicates that the operation attempted to be performed is not allowed. This could be because:
  - The api token is invalid
  - The user does not have the necessary permissions

# Content-Type: application/json
# 404
# Description: This error is returned when a resource is not found. This could be because:
  - The endpoint does not exist
  - The resource does not exist eg model id, dataset id

# Content-Type: application/json
# 422
# Description: This error is returned when the request is not well formed. This could be because:
  - JSON is invalid
  - The request is missing required fields
  - The request contains an invalid combination of fields

# Content-Type: application/json

# Content-Type: application/json

# Description: This error is returned when an uncategorised internal server error occurs.

Examples.Title: Embed API (v2)
Examples.Description:
This endpoint returns text embeddings. An embedding is a list of floating point numbers that captures semantic information about the text that it represents.

Embeddings can be used to create text classifiers as well as empower semantic search. To learn more about embeddings, see the embedding page.

If you want to learn more how to use the embedding model, have a look at the [Semantic Search Guide](https://docs.cohere.com/docs/semantic-search).
Examples.Code:
### Embed API (v2)
# @name embedv2
POST {{host}}/v2/embed
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
X-Client-Name: my-cool-project
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

{
  "texts": [
    "string"
  ],
  "images": [
    "string"
  ],
  "model": "string",
  "input_type": "search_document",
  "inputs": [
    {
      "content": []
    }
  ],
  "max_tokens": 0,
  "output_dimension": 0,
  "embedding_types": [
    "float"
  ],
  "truncate": "END",
  "priority": 0
}

## Responses
# 200
# Description: OK
# Content-Type: application/json
# 400

  - JSON is invalid
  - The request is missing required fields
  - The request contains an invalid combination of fields

Examples.Title: Rerank API (v2)
Examples.Description: This endpoint takes in a query and a list of texts and produces an ordered array with each text assigned a relevance score.
Examples.Code:
### Rerank API (v2)
# @name rerankv2
POST {{host}}/v2/rerank
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
X-Client-Name: my-cool-project
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

{
  "model": "string",
  "query": "string",
  "documents": [
    "string"
  ],
  "top_n": 0,
  "max_tokens_per_doc": 0,
  "priority": 0
}
